user_message: Pulling in context from Lucerne Publishing's escalation email -- need to generate the watchlist for the renewal risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 13:31 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing renewal risk

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 13:20 EST
Subject: URGENT: renewal risk Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 08:28 EST
Subject: URGENT: renewal risk Issue at Lucerne Publishing

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-B trap (Invents replacement-candidate); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-B failure mode (Invents replacement-candidate)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
